1: The Gog oracle opens with the standard word-event formula — and then addresses the most mysterious figure in Ezekiel's prophecy. The oracle's placement after Ezekiel 36-37 (restoration promises) and before Ezekiel 40-48 (the temple vision) situates it as the climactic threat before the final rest: the restored Israel will face a last assault before the permanent sanctuary is established.
2: ben-adam sim panecha el-Gog eretz Magog nesi rosh Meshech veTuval ('son of man, set your face toward Gog, the land of Magog, the chief prince of Meshech and Tubal'): Gog (H1463) — the personal name of the eschatological adversary; Magog — the land/people (Genesis 10:2, son of Japheth). The genealogical connection to Japheth's descendants locates Gog among the northern peoples. nesi rosh ('chief prince' or 'prince of Rosh') — the title that identifies Gog as the paramount political-military ruler of the northern peoples. Meshech and Tubal reappear from 27:13 and Ezekiel 32:26 (peoples associated with Asia Minor).
3: hineni elecha Gog ('I am against you, Gog'): the divine confrontation formula — only YHWH can address the eschatological adversary in the second person. The same formula used against Pharaoh (Ezekiel 29:3; Ezekiel 30:22) and the other OAN figures is here directed at the mysterious Gog. The hineni elecha ('I am against you') is the most direct expression of divine opposition in Ezekiel's vocabulary.
4: veshavti otach venatethi chchachim bilichyeycha ('I will turn you around and put hooks in your jaws'): the hooks-in-jaws image again — used for Pharaoh in Ezekiel 29:4. YHWH driving the great adversary into position is the theological claim that the Gog invasion is itself part of the divine plan. vetzotzeti otach ('I will lead you out') — YHWH as the one who brings Gog against Israel, making the attack itself the vehicle of divine judgment. vechal-cheylecha susim uphashim ('all your army, horses and horsemen'): the great military coalition Gog assembles.
5: The coalition: Paras (Persia/Iran), Cush (Ethiopia), Put (Libya/North Africa). The same African coalition as appeared in the Egypt oracles (Ezekiel 30:5) now assembled under Gog. The eschatological army draws on the same imperial geography as Ezekiel's historical oracles — all the great powers of the known world assembled against restored Israel.
6: Gomer and Beit-Togarma from the northern quarters — the peoples from the far north. Gomer (H1586, Cimmerians in Greek; Genesis 10:2-3, son of Japheth); Beth-Togarmah (north of the Euphrates, probably Armenia). The comprehensive directional coverage: south (Persia, Cush, Put) and north (Gomer, Beth-Togarmah) — a global coalition against the tiny restored Israel.
7: hikkon vehachen lecha atah vekhol-qahalcha haniqhalim alecha ('be prepared and ready — you and all the company assembled around you'): YHWH addressing Gog as if giving military orders — the ironic military briefing of the adversary whom YHWH is actually positioning for destruction. vehayta lahem lemishmar ('be their guard'): Gog as the guardian/commander of the coalition assembled against Israel.
8: meyamim rabbim tipaqed ('after many days you will be called up'): paqad (H6485, attend to/visit/muster — often used for divine inspection or military muster). beacharit hashanim ('in the latter years'): acharit hashanim (H319 + H8141, end of years/latter years) — the eschatological time-marker. tavo el-aretz meshovevet mecherevin niqubbetzet al-harei yisrael ('you will come against a land restored from the sword, gathered from many peoples, on the mountains of Israel'): the attack is explicitly against the restored Israel of Ezekiel 36-37 — the returned exiles in their homeland are the target of the eschatological assault.
9: vaalita kashoah tavo ('you will come up like a storm'): shoah (H7722, devastating storm/catastrophe/tempest) — the overwhelming force of the eschatological assault. keanav lechassot haaretz tihyeh atah vekhol-agafecha ('like a cloud covering the land you and all your troops'): the storm-cloud imagery for a vast army covering the land. The darkness of the cloud-army against the land of restored Israel is the visible manifestation of the eschatological threat.
10: veaalah devarim al-levavecha vechashabta machashevet raah ('thoughts will arise in your mind and you will devise an evil plan'): the interior motivation of Gog is exposed — evil machashevet (H4284, thoughts/devices/plans). The evil plan arises from Gog's own heart, yet it is simultaneously within YHWH's sovereign orchestration (Ezekiel 38:4: YHWH puts hooks in Gog's jaws). The dual agency of evil instruments serving divine purposes is sustained throughout these oracles.
11: veamarta e'eleh el-eretz perazot ('I will go up against the land of unwalled villages'): perazot (H6519, unwalled/open settlements) — Israel dwelling in security without walls or bars or gates. The vulnerability of the restored Israel is its openness: a people dwelling in safety, no walls, no defenses. The attack on the open villages is the attack on trust in YHWH's protection rather than in military fortification.
12: livzoz baz ulishlol shalal ('to plunder and loot'): the double alliteration in Hebrew — baz (H957, plunder) + shalal (H7998, spoil/booty). The economic motivation of the attack: the accumulated wealth of the restored Israel is the target. al-am mesuphaf mitoch haaratzot ('on a people gathered from the nations'): the exiles-returned, now wealthy from agricultural restoration (Ezekiel 36:29-30), are the economic target. qinyanim veqonyeh ('goods and possessions') — the tangible prosperity of the covenant restoration becomes the bait that draws Gog.
13: The trading nations — Sheba, Dedan, merchants of Tarshish — question whether Gog is coming to plunder (showing commercial awareness of the attack). These are the same trading nations of Ezekiel 27:20-22 (Ezekiel's Tyre oracle). Their question is not opposition but commercial interest: halasheset baz attah ba? ('have you come to plunder?'). The merchants of the world are watching the eschatological assault with commercial calculation rather than moral horror.
14: lachen hinabbe ben-adam veamarta leGog ('therefore prophesy, son of man, and say to Gog'): Ezekiel is commissioned to prophesy directly to Gog — the eschatological adversary as the object of prophetic address, just as historical nations received the OAN oracles. beyom shevet ami yisrael lavetach halo teda ('on the day my people Israel dwell in safety, will you not take notice?'): YHWH asking Gog rhetorically — the secure settlement of Israel is both the trigger for Gog's attack and the sign of YHWH's restoration.
15: ubata mimqomcha miyrektei tzaphon ('you will come from your place out of the remote parts of the north'): yarktei tzaphon (H3411 + H6828, remote parts/recesses of the north) — the north as the direction of mythic threat (cf. Jeremiah 1:14 'out of the north evil will break out'; Isaiah 14:13 'mountain of assembly in the far north'). The cosmic north as the direction of the final assault. ammim rabbim itach ('many peoples with you'): the vast multinational coalition of Ezekiel 38:5-6.
16: vaalita al-ami yisrael ke'anan lechassot haaretz ('you will go up against my people Israel like a cloud covering the land'): the cloud imagery of Ezekiel 38:9 repeated — the storm-cloud assault against restored Israel. beacharit hayamim yihyeh ('in the latter days it will be'): the eschatological time-marker (acharit hayamim, H319 + H3117) — the time when the present age reaches its completion. veheveti otcha al-artzi ('I will bring you against my land'): YHWH explicitly orchestrating Gog's attack against his own land — the assault serves YHWH's purpose of displaying his holiness to the nations. lemaan deathi haggoyim bi lehiqqdashi vecha Gog leeyneichem ('so that the nations may know me when I show myself holy through you, Gog').
17: hata attah hu asher dibberi biyamim qadmonim beyad avadai neviiei yisrael ('are you the one about whom I spoke in earlier days through my servants the prophets of Israel?'): YHWH's self-referential query — connecting the Gog oracle to earlier prophetic traditions (Isaiah 14:25-27; Jeremiah 4:5-13; Zephaniah 3:8). The eschatological adversary was anticipated by earlier prophecy; Ezekiel's oracle is the explicit fulfillment of those earlier references. This verse places the Gog material within the canonical tradition of eschatological judgment.
18: vehayah bayyom hahu beyyom bo Gog al-admat yisrael ('on that day, when Gog comes against the land of Israel'): bayyom hahu — the eschatological 'on that day' formula. yaale chamati vaappi ('my wrath will flare up'): chemah (H2534, wrath/heat/fury) + ap (H639, nose/anger) — the classic pair of divine anger vocabulary. beqin'ati vebe'esh evrati ('in my jealousy and in the fire of my wrath'): three anger words — qin'ah (jealousy/zeal), chemah (wrath), evrah (H5678, rage/fury) — piled up to convey the intensity of the divine response to the final assault on restored Israel.
19: vebiqqin'ati vebe'esh evrati dibbari ('in my jealousy and blazing wrath I declare'): the formal declaration of the eschatological judgment. im-lo bayyom hahu yihyeh ra'ash gadol al-admat yisrael ('there will be a great earthquake in the land of Israel on that day'): ra'ash (H7494, earthquake/shaking/commotion) — the cosmic shaking that accompanies YHWH's intervention against Gog. The earthquake is not merely geological but theophanic — YHWH's physical presence disturbing the created order.
20: vera'ashu mipenai degei hayam veoph hashamayim vechayat hassadeh ('the fish of the sea, the birds of the sky, and the beasts of the field will tremble before me'): the cosmic trembling extends to the entire animal kingdom — the earthquake disrupts the entire created order. vekhol-adam asher al-penei haaretz ('and all the people on the face of the earth'): universal human trembling at the theophany. veniharsu heharim venaphelu hamaqdshot ('the mountains will be thrown down and the cliffs will fall'): the topographical disruption — the mountains of Israel that received the restoration promises now shake at the divine self-disclosure.
21: veqarati alav lekol-harai cherev ('I will call for a sword against him throughout all my mountains'): YHWH summoning the sword throughout his own land — the mountains of Israel where Gog's forces are deployed become the site of their destruction. cherev ish beachiv tihyeh ('every man's sword will be against his brother'): the coalition's internal collapse through friendly fire — the multinational army turns on itself, the confusion of the divine judgment causing fratricide among the allied forces.
22: venishpatti itto bedever uvdad vegeshem shotef veavaney elgavish ('I will execute judgment on him with pestilence and bloodshed; I will rain down torrents of rain, hailstones, fire and brimstone'): the five instruments of divine judgment against Gog — pestilence, blood, rain, hail, fire and brimstone. The last two (fire and brimstone) recall Sodom's destruction (Genesis 19:24) — the primordial judgment city-destroying event applied to the eschatological adversary. elgavish (H68 + H1378, brimstone/sulfur hailstones) — the specific word for the sulfurous hail of divine judgment.
23: vehitgaddilti vehitqaddishti venodaati le-eynei goyim rabbim ('I will show my greatness and my holiness and make myself known in the eyes of many nations'): the recognition formula at maximum scope — not just one nation but goyim rabbim (many nations) will know YHWH through the Gog judgment. veyadu ki-ani YHWH: the Ezekielian recognition formula closes the chapter, as it closes every major unit. The entire Gog oracle, like all of Ezekiel's oracles, serves this one epistemological purpose: YHWH's self-disclosure.
